action.reasoning: Observation: baseline ConvNeXt-Tiny reached 0.7881 test accuracy after only 4 epochs in ~3.5 minutes training, so pipeline is solid and there is ample budget. Analysis: with 40% instance-dependent noise, the next likely gains come from stronger robustness rather than just longer training. A practical low-risk upgrade is two-stage training: first train a decent model, then use its predictions to relabel/filter low-confidence noisy samples and fine-tune on a mixed hard/cleaner target. This is simpler than full co-teaching and should fit the budget. Decision: extend train.py to support a self-distillation/bootstrapping stage with per-sample pseudo-labels and confidence-based mixing, train for a moderate number of epochs, test predict locally, and submit the improved checkpoint.
action.command: python train.py --epochs_stage1 6 --epochs_stage2 6 --batch_size 64 --lr 3e-4 --lr_stage2 1.5e-4 --mix_alpha 0.7 --conf_power 2.0 --out checkpoints/selfdistill.pt && python predict.py --data_path /data/val.pt --checkpoint checkpoints/selfdistill.pt --output smoke_preds.json
